select.fastdom.fastdom.name: fastdom
select.fastdom.fastdom.description:
Batches DOM reads and writes to reduce layout thrashing. This module is a
FastDOM scheduling pattern that exposes a shared instance for queueing
measurement and mutation work.

select.fastdom.FastDOM.name: FastDOM
select.fastdom.FastDOM.description:
Scheduler that batches `measure` and `mutate` work into animation frames.
-

members.name: raf
members.description: frame scheduler used to trigger flushes

select.fastdom.flush.name: flush
select.fastdom.flush.description:
Runs queued read tasks before write tasks for `fastdom`, rescheduling when
pending work remains after an error.

select.fastdom.remove.name: remove
select.fastdom.remove.description: Removes `item` from `array` and returns whether the array changed.

select.fastdom.mixin.name: mixin
select.fastdom.mixin.description: Copies own enumerable properties from `source` into `target`.

select.routing.router.name: router
select.routing.router.description: Creates a `Router` and registers all entries from `routes`.

select.routing.routed.name: routed

select.ui.webcomponents.webcomponents.name: webcomponents
select.ui.webcomponents.webcomponents.description: Web component bridge for UI templates and pure render functions.

select.ui.webcomponents.UIWebComponent.name: UIWebComponent
select.ui.webcomponents.UIWebComponent.description:
Base custom element that binds a component factory to DOM attributes and
renders its template into `root`.

select.ui.webcomponents.webcomponent.name: webcomponent
select.ui.webcomponents.webcomponent.description:
Defines and returns a custom element class bound to `componentFactory`.
Initializes attributes from `initial` and optional mapping in `options`.
Plain values in `initial` become default data, while function values act as
attribute processors for the matching attribute names.

select.ui.ui.name: ui
select.ui.ui.description: Re-export surface for the UI runtime.

select.ui.remap.name: remap
select.ui.remap.description: Maps `f` over collection entries while preserving the input container shape.

select.ui.templates.templates.name: templates
select.ui.templates.templates.description: HTML template parsing, data path parsing, and DOM binding helpers.

select.ui.templates.setNodeText.name: setNodeText
select.ui.templates.setNodeText.description: Applies `text` to a text node or form-like element value in place.

select.ui.components.components.name: components
select.ui.components.components.description:
Public barrel for the component runtime. Re-exports the component model,
registry helpers, template instance, template class, and slot descriptors.

select.ui.components.runtime.runtime.name: runtime
select.ui.components.runtime.runtime.description: Shared rendering helpers for component templates and instances.

select.ui.components.model.model.name: model
select.ui.components.model.model.description: Small model classes shared by the component runtime.

select.ui.components.registry.registry.name: registry
select.ui.components.registry.registry.description: Component registry, module-level options, and dynamic component helpers.

select.ui.components.registry.Dynamic.name: Dynamic
select.ui.components.registry.Dynamic.description:
Resolves `type` from the component registry when needed and invokes it with
`props`. Returns `null` when no component matches.

select.ui.components.registry.lazy.name: lazy
select.ui.components.registry.lazy.description:
Wraps async `loader` and returns a component function that renders
`placeholder` until the loaded template is available.

select.ui.components.slots.slots.name: slots
select.ui.components.slots.slots.description: Template slot descriptors and mounted slot renderers.

select.ui.components.slots.UIAttributeTe.name: UIAttributeTemplateSlot
select.ui.components.slots.UIAttributeTe.description: Template slot for attribute binding (e.g., out:style, out:class).

select.ui.components.slots.UIEventTempla.name: UIEventTemplateSlot
select.ui.components.slots.UIEventTempla.description: Template slot for event binding (e.g., on:click).

select.ui.components.slots.UISlot.name: UISlot
select.ui.components.slots.UISlot.description:
Manages dynamic content rendering in an output slot. Handles lists,
dictionaries, applied templates, and plain values.

select.ui.components.slots.UIContentSlot.name: UIContentSlot
select.ui.components.slots.UIContentSlot.description:
Manages a named  element's content, handling fallback and provided
content with efficient diffing.

select.ui.components.template.template.name: template
select.ui.components.template.template.description: Reusable UI template class and template mapping helpers.

select.ui.components.template.UITemplate.name: UITemplate
select.ui.components.template.UITemplate.description:
Defines a reusable UI template parsed from HTML. Discovers slots, bindings,
and content slots. Provides factory methods for creating instances.

select.ui.components.instance.instance.name: instance
select.ui.components.instance.instance.description: Mounted UI template instances, lifecycle, events, and rendering.

select.ui.components.instance.UIInstance.name: UIInstance
select.ui.components.instance.UIInstance.description:
A mounted instance of a UITemplate. Manages data binding, event handling,
lifecycle, and rendering.

select.ui.factory.factory.name: factory
select.ui.factory.factory.description: UI component factory and template/component construction.

select.ui.factory.createComponent.name: createComponent
select.ui.factory.createComponent.description:
Wraps a `UITemplate` into a callable component facade and attaches helper
methods (`new`, `map`, `on`, `sub`, `cleanup`) used by the UI runtime.

select.ui.factory.ui.name: ui
select.ui.factory.ui.description:
Resolves `selection` against `scope` and returns a component factory.
`selection` can be HTML, a CSS selector, a DOM node, or an array of nodes.

select.interaction.bind.name: bind
select.interaction.bind.description:
Registers `handlers` on `node`. Accepts a single node or an array-like
collection of nodes and returns the original `node`.

select.interaction.unbind.name: unbind
select.interaction.unbind.description:
Removes `handlers` from `node`. Accepts a single node or an array-like
collection of nodes and returns the original `node`.

select.interaction.drag.name: drag
select.interaction.drag.description:
Starts a drag interaction on `event.target` and invokes `move` and `end`
callbacks with a shared drag context.

select.interaction.target.name: target
select.interaction.target.description: Walks up from `node` until `pred(node)` returns true.

select.interaction.dragtarget.name: dragtarget
select.interaction.dragtarget.description:
Walks up from `node` to find a `data-drag` target, optionally matching
`name`.

select.interaction.autoresize.name: autoresize
select.interaction.autoresize.description: Resizes a textarea-like `event.target` to fit its scroll height.

select.utils.logger.logger.name: logger
select.utils.logger.logger.description: Returns scoped logging methods (`log`, `warn`, `error`) prefixed with `scope`.

select.utils.sanitize.sanitize.name: sanitize
select.utils.sanitize.sanitize.description: Sanitizes `value` using the resolved sanitizer for `options`.

select.utils.sanitize.Sanitizer.name: Sanitizer
select.utils.sanitize.Sanitizer.description:
Sanitizes arrays and plain objects while allowing hooks for keys, text, and scalar values.
- dropUndefined: 
- compactArrays: 
- keepNonPlainObjects: 
- sanitizeTextHook: function | 
- sanitizeKeyHook: function | 
- sanitizeScalarHook: function | 
- onDrop: function |

members.name: boolean
members.description: Removes dropped array entries when true.

select.utils.sanitize.sanitizer.name: sanitizer
select.utils.sanitize.sanitizer.description: Returns a cached sanitizer instance for `options`.

select.utils.text.text.name: text
select.utils.text.text.description: Text compression, highlighting, and regular-expression helpers.

select.utils.text._words.name: _words
select.utils.text._words.description: Splits mixed-case, kebab-case, snake_case, and spaced `value` into words.

select.utils.text.asNumber.name: asNumber
select.utils.text.asNumber.description: Coerces numeric-like values into a number, defaulting to `0`.

select.utils.text.re.name: re
select.utils.text.re.description: Returns a cached regular expression for string `query`.

select.utils.text.reducematches.name: reducematches
select.utils.text.reducematches.description: Reduces `text` and regexp matches through `reducer`, including unmatched spans.

select.utils.text.iresplit.name: iresplit
select.utils.text.iresplit.description: Splits the given `text` based on the regular expression `re`.

select.utils.text.safekey.name: safekey
select.utils.text.safekey.description: Normalizes arbitrary text into a key-safe token separated by `sep`.

select.utils.html.html.name: html
select.utils.html.html.description: HTML-oriented helpers for class-name normalization and text coercion.

select.utils.html.asText.name: asText
select.utils.html.asText.description: Converts `value` to displayable text, expanding reactive values.

select.utils.html.iclsx.name: iclsx
select.utils.html.iclsx.description: Generator yielding normalized class tokens from mixed inputs.

select.utils.html.clsx.name: clsx
select.utils.html.clsx.description: Joins normalized class tokens into a single class string.

select.utils.html.hi.name: hi
select.utils.html.hi.description: Highlights `query` matches in `text` and returns a text node or fragment.

select.utils.compare.eq.name: eq
select.utils.compare.eq.description: Performs deep structural equality on arrays, plain objects, maps, and sets.

select.utils.compare.cmp.name: cmp
select.utils.compare.cmp.description: Compares `a` and `b`, optionally after extractor projection.

select.utils.math.math.name: math
select.utils.math.math.description:
Numeric helper functions for interpolation, ranges, distances, and summary
statistics. Collection-aware helpers use Select.js list/reduce semantics.

select.utils.math.extreme.name: extreme
select.utils.math.extreme.description: Returns the extreme scalar found in `args` using reducer `func`.

select.utils.math.isNumericList.name: isNumericList
select.utils.math.isNumericList.description: Returns true when `value` should be treated as an indexable numeric list.

select.utils.math.unary.name: unary
select.utils.math.unary.description: Applies unary numeric operator `func` to scalar or list-like `value`.

select.utils.math.binary.name: binary
select.utils.math.binary.description: Applies binary numeric operator `func` to scalars or pairwise list-like values.

select.utils.math.cos.name: cos
select.utils.math.cos.description: Returns the cosine of `value` in radians.

select.utils.math.sin.name: sin
select.utils.math.sin.description: Returns the sine of `value` in radians.

select.utils.math.sind.name: sind
select.utils.math.sind.description: Returns the sine of `value` in degrees.

select.utils.math.cosd.name: cosd
select.utils.math.cosd.description: Returns the cosine of `value` in degrees.

select.utils.math.pis.name: pis
select.utils.math.pis.description: Converts `value` in radians to a factor of pi.

select.utils.math.radiald.name: radiald
select.utils.math.radiald.description: Returns `[x, y]` coordinates for degree `angle` and `radius`.

select.utils.math.sign.name: sign
select.utils.math.sign.description: Returns `-1` for negative values and `1` otherwise.

select.utils.math.order.name: order
select.utils.math.order.description: Returns the logarithmic order of magnitude of `value` in `base`.

select.utils.math.clamp.name: clamp
select.utils.math.clamp.description: Clamps `value` to the inclusive range `[min, max]`.

select.utils.math.abs.name: abs
select.utils.math.abs.description: Returns the absolute value of `value`, preserving list shape when possible.

select.utils.math.floor.name: floor
select.utils.math.floor.description: Returns the floor of `value`, preserving list shape when possible.

select.utils.math.ceil.name: ceil
select.utils.math.ceil.description: Returns the ceil of `value`, preserving list shape when possible.

select.utils.math.round.name: round
select.utils.math.round.description: Rounds `value` to `factor`; `bound` selects floor `(-1)`, nearest `(0)`, or ceil `(1)`.

select.utils.math.add.name: add
select.utils.math.add.description: Adds `a` and `b`, or sums a single list-like `a`.

select.utils.math.sub.name: sub
select.utils.math.sub.description: Subtracts `b` from `a`, or subtracts the first two values of pair `a`.

select.utils.math.mul.name: mul
select.utils.math.mul.description: Multiplies `a` and `b`, or multiplies every value in list-like `a`.

select.utils.math.div.name: div
select.utils.math.div.description: Divides `a` by `b`, preserving list shape when `a` is list-like.

select.utils.math.lerp.name: lerp
select.utils.math.lerp.description: Linearly interpolates between `a` and `b` using factor `t`.

select.utils.math.prel.name: prel
select.utils.math.prel.description: Converts `t` from the interval `[a, b]` into a normalized ratio.

select.utils.math.smoothstep.name: smoothstep
select.utils.math.smoothstep.description: Applies cubic smoothstep easing to normalized `value`.

select.utils.math.smootherstep.name: smootherstep
select.utils.math.smootherstep.description: Applies quintic smootherstep easing to normalized `value`.

select.utils.math.range.name: range
select.utils.math.range.description: Returns values from `start` to `end` using `step`; when `closed`, includes `end`.

select.utils.math.subdivide.name: subdivide
select.utils.math.subdivide.description: Splits `[start, end]` into `steps` evenly spaced values.

select.utils.math.dist.name: dist
select.utils.math.dist.description: Returns the absolute distance between `a` and `b`.

select.utils.math.reldist.name: reldist
select.utils.math.reldist.description: Returns the signed relative distance from `a` to `b`.

select.utils.math.circdist.name: circdist
select.utils.math.circdist.description: Returns the shortest circular distance between normalized values `a` and `b`.

select.utils.math.within.name: within
select.utils.math.within.description: Returns true when `value` is within inclusive range `[min, max]`.

select.utils.math.closestInOrder.name: closestInOrder
select.utils.math.closestInOrder.description: Returns the nearest value to `value` in ascending `values`; `affinity` biases ties.

select.utils.math.closest.name: closest
select.utils.math.closest.description: Returns the closest entry to `value` in `values`, or the closest multiple of `b` to scalar `a`.

select.utils.math.sqdist.name: sqdist
select.utils.math.sqdist.description: Returns the squared distance between `a` and `b`.

select.utils.math.min.name: min
select.utils.math.min.description: Returns the minimum scalar across `args`, descending into list-like values.

select.utils.math.max.name: max
select.utils.math.max.description: Returns the maximum scalar across `args`, descending into list-like values.

select.utils.math.extent.name: extent
select.utils.math.extent.description: Returns the absolute span between minimum and maximum values in `series`.

select.utils.math.midpoint.name: midpoint
select.utils.math.midpoint.description: Returns the midpoint between the minimum and maximum values in `series`.

select.utils.math.rmsd.name: rmsd
select.utils.math.rmsd.description: Returns the root mean square deviation of `series` from `target`.

select.utils.math.factorial.name: factorial
select.utils.math.factorial.description: Returns the factorial of non-negative integer `n`.

select.utils.math.cnk.name: cnk
select.utils.math.cnk.description: Returns the binomial coefficient for `n` choose `k`.

select.utils.math.random.name: random
select.utils.math.random.description: Returns a random value in `[0, 1)`, `[0, lower)`, or `[lower, upper)`.

select.utils.math.mod.name: mod
select.utils.math.mod.description: Returns mathematical modulo instead of JavaScript remainder.

select.utils.math.between.name: between
select.utils.math.between.description: Returns true when `value` is between `a` and `b` using inclusive bounds by default.

select.utils.math.contained.name: contained
select.utils.math.contained.description: Returns true when range `[a0, a1]` is fully contained within `[b0, b1]`.

select.utils.math.overlaps.name: overlaps
select.utils.math.overlaps.description: Returns true when ranges `[a0, a1]` and `[b0, b1]` overlap.

select.utils.math.roundsum.name: roundsum
select.utils.math.roundsum.description: Scales and rounds `series` so its total matches `total`.

select.utils.math.nice.name: nice
select.utils.math.nice.description: Returns a human-friendly rounded value near `value`.

select.utils.math.step.name: step
select.utils.math.step.description: Returns a convenient step size for `[start, end]`.

select.utils.math.steps.name: steps
select.utils.math.steps.description: Returns a scale of convenient values from `start` to `end`.

select.utils.math.nicer.name: nicer
select.utils.math.nicer.description: Snaps `value` to a nearby pleasant round number from normalized `values`.

select.utils.update.prepend.name: prepend
select.utils.update.prepend.description: Mutably inserts `item` at the beginning of `collection`.

select.utils.update.assign.name: assign
select.utils.update.assign.description: Mutably assigns `value` at path `p` in `scope` and returns the root container.

select.utils.selection.selection.name: selection
select.utils.selection.selection.description:
Selection helpers for item lookup, membership, mutation, and circular
navigation.

select.utils.selection.itemkey.name: itemkey
select.utils.selection.itemkey.description: Resolves a stable key from common item fields (`id`, `key`, `name`).

select.utils.selection.items.name: items
select.utils.selection.items.description: Normalizes `items` by wrapping primitive values into `{ label, value }`.

select.utils.selection.index.name: index
select.utils.selection.index.description: Returns the index of `item` in `items` using `key` matching when provided.

select.utils.selection.add.name: add
select.utils.selection.add.description: Adds `item` to `items` if not already present.

select.utils.selection.next.name: next
select.utils.selection.next.description: Returns next wrapped index from `index` by `delta`.

select.utils.selection.wrapindex.name: wrapindex
select.utils.selection.wrapindex.description: Wraps index arithmetic over `itemsOrLength` with circular behavior.

select.utils.iter.iter.name: iter
select.utils.iter.iter.description:
Iteration helpers for list-like, map-like, and scalar values. Includes
`iquery()` path expansion with `*` wildcard segments.

select.utils.iter.iquery.name: iquery
select.utils.iter.iquery.description:
Queries `value` with `path` and yields matching terminal values. Wildcard
segments use a trailing `*` suffix and expand the referenced collection.

select.utils.iter.ivalues.name: ivalues
select.utils.iter.ivalues.description: Yields values for array-like, map-like, iterable, and scalar inputs.

select.utils.iter.iflatmap.name: iflatmap
select.utils.iter.iflatmap.description: Maps `values` with `func` and yields flattened results one level deep.

select.utils.iter.iitems.name: iitems
select.utils.iter.iitems.description: Yields `[key, value]` pairs for array-like, map-like, iterable, and scalar values.

select.utils.iter.ikeys.name: ikeys
select.utils.iter.ikeys.description: Yields keys for array-like, map-like, iterable, and scalar values.

select.utils.iter.iindex.name: iindex
select.utils.iter.iindex.description: Returns the first index or key whose value strictly equals `item`.

select.utils.iter.ifind.name: ifind
select.utils.iter.ifind.description: Returns the first index or key whose value satisfies `predicate`.

select.utils.iter.ifirst.name: ifirst
select.utils.iter.ifirst.description: Returns the first value in `values`, optionally matching `predicate`.

select.utils.iter.ilast.name: ilast
select.utils.iter.ilast.description: Returns the last value in `values`, optionally matching `predicate`.

select.utils.iter.inth.name: inth
select.utils.iter.inth.description: Returns the value at `index`, supporting negative indexes.

select.utils.iter.icount.name: icount
select.utils.iter.icount.description: Counts all values or values matching a predicate.

select.utils.iter.ifound.name: ifound
select.utils.iter.ifound.description: Returns the first value equal to `item`, optionally by projection.

select.utils.iter.ihead.name: ihead
select.utils.iter.ihead.description: Returns the first item or first `count` items from `values`.

select.utils.iter.ientries.name: ientries
select.utils.iter.ientries.description: Returns collection entries as `[key, value]` pairs.

select.utils.iter.iremap.name: iremap
select.utils.iter.iremap.description:
Streams `[path, new, old]` updates for the given `(new,old)` values. When
path is `null` it means the whole value, when `new` is `null` it means
a removal, when `old` is undefined, it means an addition.

select.utils.iter.iwalk.name: iwalk
select.utils.iter.iwalk.description: Generator that depth-walks `value`, yielding `[node, path, parents]` tuples.

select.utils.iter.ileaves.name: ileaves
select.utils.iter.ileaves.description: Generator that depth-walks `value` and yields only leaf values.

select.utils.traverse.traverse.name: traverse
select.utils.traverse.traverse.description: Read-only traversal, lookup, path access, and recursive remapping helpers.

select.utils.traverse.path.name: path
select.utils.traverse.path.description: Normalizes `p` to a path array, returning `null` when unset.

select.utils.traverse.iter.name: iter
select.utils.traverse.iter.description: Iterates `value` and optionally finalizes the accumulator with `processor`.

select.utils.traverse.each.name: each
select.utils.traverse.each.description: Applies `func` to every collection value and returns the original value.

select.utils.traverse.get.name: get
select.utils.traverse.get.description: Returns the value at `key`; arrays may be used as nested paths.

select.utils.traverse.has.name: has
select.utils.traverse.has.description: Returns true when `parent` has `key`; arrays may be used as nested paths.

select.utils.traverse.index.name: index
select.utils.traverse.index.description: Returns the first index or key whose value strictly equals `item`.

select.utils.traverse.find.name: find
select.utils.traverse.find.description: Returns the first index or key whose value satisfies `predicate`.

select.utils.traverse.first.name: first
select.utils.traverse.first.description: Returns the first value in `values`, optionally matching `predicate`.

select.utils.traverse.last.name: last
select.utils.traverse.last.description: Returns the last value in `values`, optionally matching `predicate`.

select.utils.traverse.nth.name: nth
select.utils.traverse.nth.description: Returns the value at `index`, supporting negative indexes.

select.utils.traverse.count.name: count
select.utils.traverse.count.description: Counts all values or values matching a predicate.

select.utils.traverse.found.name: found
select.utils.traverse.found.description: Returns the first value equal to `item`, optionally by projection.

select.utils.traverse.isin.name: isin
select.utils.traverse.isin.description: Returns true when `value` is present in `values`.

select.utils.traverse.head.name: head
select.utils.traverse.head.description: Returns the first item or first `count` items from `values`.

select.utils.traverse.entries.name: entries
select.utils.traverse.entries.description: Returns collection entries as `[key, value]` pairs.

select.utils.traverse.remap.name: remap
select.utils.traverse.remap.description: Maps `value` with `mapper`; supports deep traversal with path-aware options.

select.utils.search.search.name: search
select.utils.search.search.description:
Predicate combinators and text-search helpers.
`match` resolves a criteria value against a candidate. Criteria can be
primitives, regular expressions, arrays, object shapes, or predicate
functions. `textfilter` builds a search regexp from a query string using
`OR` clauses, quoted phrases, and a `?` wildcard token.

examples.code:
```javascript
import search from "./utils/search.js"
const filter = search.text("alpha OR \"beta gamma\"")
filter.test("alpha") // true
```

select.utils.search.donot.name: donot
select.utils.search.donot.description: Returns the negated result of `match(value, criteria)`.

select.utils.search.or.name: or
select.utils.search.or.description: Returns a predicate that matches `value` when any of `criteria` match.

select.utils.search.and.name: and
select.utils.search.and.description: Returns a predicate that matches `value` when all of `criteria` match.

select.utils.search.match.name: match
select.utils.search.match.description:
Returns `true` when `value` satisfies `criteria`.
- Falsy criteria matches everything.
- `RegExp` criteria test the string form of `value`.
- Array criteria require all items to match.
- Object criteria require all enumerable keys to match recursively.
- Function criteria are called with `value` and should return a boolean.
- Other criteria are matched by strict identity.

select.utils.search.textfilter.name: textfilter
select.utils.search.textfilter.description:
Builds a case-insensitive regular expression from `text`.
`OR` splits alternatives, quoted terms are treated as exact phrases, and
`?` expands to an optional whitespace-delimited capture.

examples.code:
```javascript
const re = textfilter('alpha OR "beta gamma"')
re.test("beta gamma") // true
```

select.utils.search.predicate.name: predicate
select.utils.search.predicate.description:
Returns a predicate for `criteria`.
- No criteria returns `null`.
- One criterion returns a unary matcher using `match`.
- Multiple criteria return an `and` combinator.

select.utils.transform.transform.name: transform
select.utils.transform.transform.description: Pure collection transforms and immutable structural update helpers.

select.utils.transform.map.name: map
select.utils.transform.map.description: Maps collection values while preserving array, object, map, and set shape.

select.utils.transform.reduce.name: reduce
select.utils.transform.reduce.description: Reduces collection values with `(result, value, key)` semantics.

select.utils.transform.updated.name: updated
select.utils.transform.updated.description: Returns a copy of `value` with `other` assigned at `key`.

select.utils.transform.swapped.name: swapped
select.utils.transform.swapped.description: Returns a copy of `value` with entries at `keya` and `keyb` swapped.

select.utils.transform.sorted.name: sorted
select.utils.transform.sorted.description: Returns sorted copy of `values`, optionally by projection and ordering.

select.utils.transform.unique.name: unique
select.utils.transform.unique.description: Returns unique values from `values`, optionally by projection, while preserving shape.

select.utils.transform.filter.name: filter
select.utils.transform.filter.description: Filters `values` using resolved predicate semantics while preserving shape.

select.utils.transform.flatmap.name: flatmap
select.utils.transform.flatmap.description: Maps `values` with `func` and flattens mapped results one level deep.

select.utils.transform.prune.name: prune
select.utils.transform.prune.description: Returns values that do not match the predicate while preserving shape.

select.utils.transform.pruned.name: pruned
select.utils.transform.pruned.description: Returns a shallow copy of `value` without the given keys.

select.utils.transform.slice.name: slice
select.utils.transform.slice.description: Returns a positional slice while preserving supported container shapes.

select.utils.transform.reverse.name: reverse
select.utils.transform.reverse.description: Returns `values` in reverse order while preserving supported container shapes.

select.utils.transform.concat.name: concat
select.utils.transform.concat.description: Returns `a` with values from `b` appended, preserving `a` shape when possible.

select.utils.transform.flatten.name: flatten
select.utils.transform.flatten.description: Flattens nested collection values up to `depth`.

select.utils.transform.grouped.name: grouped
select.utils.transform.grouped.description: Groups `values` using `extractorFunc`, optionally projecting each grouped entry.

select.utils.transform.partition.name: partition
select.utils.transform.partition.description: Partitions `values` into `[matched, unmatched]` while preserving shape.

select.utils.transform.difference.name: difference
select.utils.transform.difference.description: Returns values from `a` that are not present in `b`, preserving shape.

select.utils.transform.removeAt.name: removeAt
select.utils.transform.removeAt.description: Returns `values` without the entry at `index` or `key`.

select.utils.transform.stripe.name: stripe
select.utils.transform.stripe.description: Returns values interleaved from even then odd positions while preserving shape.

select.utils.transform.enumerate.name: enumerate
select.utils.transform.enumerate.description: Returns a frozen identity dictionary for the given string values.

select.utils.transform.prepended.name: prepended
select.utils.transform.prepended.description: Returns `collection` with `item` inserted at the beginning.

select.utils.transform.appended.name: appended
select.utils.transform.appended.description: Returns `collection` with `item` inserted at the end.

select.utils.transform.copy.name: copy
select.utils.transform.copy.description: Recursively copies supported containers up to `limit` depth.

select.utils.transform.merge.name: merge
select.utils.transform.merge.description: Deeply merges matching container types; right-hand values replace scalars.

select.utils.transform.assigned.name: assigned
select.utils.transform.assigned.description: Immutably assigns `value` at path `p` by cloning touched branches.

select.utils.values.values.name: values
select.utils.values.values.description: Returns collection values as an array.

select.utils.values.isObject.name: isObject
select.utils.values.isObject.description: Returns true when `value` is a plain object.

select.utils.values.array.name: array
select.utils.values.array.description: Returns an array of `count` entries created by `creator` or their index.

select.utils.values.set.name: set
select.utils.values.set.description: Coerces `value` into a native Set using list-normalized values.

select.utils.values.keys.name: keys
select.utils.values.keys.description: Returns collection keys, indexes, or set values as an array.

select.utils.values.isMapLike.name: isMapLike
select.utils.values.isMapLike.description: Returns true when `value` exposes object-style or map-style keyed access.

select.utils.values.isArrayLike.name: isArrayLike
select.utils.values.isArrayLike.description: Returns true when `value` behaves like an index-addressable list.

select.utils.values.isWalkable.name: isWalkable
select.utils.values.isWalkable.description: Returns true when `value` should be descended into by `iwalk`.

select.utils.values.isReactive.name: isReactive
select.utils.values.isReactive.description: Returns true when `value` follows Select.js reactive value marker semantics.

select.utils.values.atom.name: atom
select.utils.values.atom.description: Returns true when `value` is a scalar value.

select.utils.values.composite.name: composite
select.utils.values.composite.description: Returns true when `value` is a supported collection container.

select.utils.values.isEmpty.name: isEmpty
select.utils.values.isEmpty.description: Returns true when `value` is an empty array, object, string, number, or missing value.

select.utils.values.bool.name: bool
select.utils.values.bool.description: Coerces `value` to semantic truthiness used by utility helpers.

select.utils.values.empty.name: empty
select.utils.values.empty.description: Returns an empty container of the same value type.

select.utils.values.freeze.name: freeze
select.utils.values.freeze.description: Freezes object values in place and returns `value` unchanged for scalars.

select.utils.values.type.name: type
select.utils.values.type.description: Returns the Select.js value kind constant for `value`.

select.utils.values.singleton.name: singleton
select.utils.values.singleton.description: Returns a shared instance of `type` using `storage`.

select.utils.values.flyweight.name: flyweight
select.utils.values.flyweight.description: Returns a recycled instance of `type` or creates a new one.

select.utils.values.recycle.name: recycle
select.utils.values.recycle.description: Resets and stores `value` for later `flyweight` reuse.

select.utils.values.clone.name: clone
select.utils.values.clone.description: Creates a shallow clone for `value`, inferring container type from `key` when needed.

select.utils.func.func.name: func
select.utils.func.func.description: Function composition, defaults, and memoization helpers.

select.utils.func.def.name: def
select.utils.func.def.description: Returns the first argument that is not `undefined`.

select.utils.func.ary.name: ary
select.utils.func.ary.description: Returns a wrapper limiting `f` to `n` call-time arguments and appending `rest`.

select.utils.func.meta.name: meta
select.utils.func.meta.description: Assigns metadata entries from `annotations` onto `value`, or reads them back.

select.utils.func.named.name: named
select.utils.func.named.description: Annotates the single `[name]:value` entry with `name` metadata and returns `value`.

select.utils.func.pipe.name: pipe
select.utils.func.pipe.description:
Applies each function in `f` to `v`, returning the final result.
Equivalent to `pipeline(...f)(v)`.

select.utils.func.pipeline.name: pipeline
select.utils.func.pipeline.description:
Returns a function applying `f` as a pipeline.
The first stage receives all arguments and later stages receive the prior result.
A single promise-like input is resolved before the first stage.

select.utils.func.memo.name: memo
select.utils.func.memo.description: Returns a cached `functor` result scoped by one or more `guards`.

select.utils.http.http.name: http
select.utils.http.http.description: Returns an `HTTPClient` instance or the shared singleton.

select.utils.http.relurl.name: relurl
select.utils.http.relurl.description: Returns `path` prefixed with `base` when `path` is relative.

select.utils.http.Throttle.name: Throttle
select.utils.http.Throttle.description:
Waits `delay` milliseconds between admitted operations.
- delay:

members.name: number
members.description: minimum delay in milliseconds between joins

select.utils.http.Throttle.number.name: number
select.utils.http.Throttle.number.description: minimum delay in milliseconds between joins

select.utils.http.Backoff.name: Backoff
select.utils.http.Backoff.description:
Computes an increasing delay across retries.
- delay: 
- factor: 
- maxDelay: 
- jitter:

select.utils.http.Retry.name: Retry
select.utils.http.Retry.description:
Retry policy that tracks how many more attempts may continue.
- retries: 
- accepts:

members.name: number
members.description: maximum number of retries after the initial request

select.utils.http.Retry.number.name: number
select.utils.http.Retry.number.description: maximum number of retries after the initial request

select.utils.http.Cache.name: Cache
select.utils.http.Cache.description:
In-memory cache keyed by URL and optional entry key.
- ttl: 
- accepts:

members.name: number
members.description: cache time-to-live in milliseconds, `0` means no expiry

select.utils.http.Cache.number.name: number
select.utils.http.Cache.number.description: cache time-to-live in milliseconds, `0` means no expiry

select.utils.http.HTTPClient.name: HTTPClient
select.utils.http.HTTPClient.description:
Fetch wrapper with URL resolution, retry policy, throttling, and caching.
- base: string|
- fetch: 
- throttle: Throttle|
- backoff: Backoff|
- retry: Retry|
- cache: Cache|object|

members.name: URL
members.description: optional base URL for relative requests

select.utils.http.HTTPClient.URL.name: URL
select.utils.http.HTTPClient.URL.description: optional base URL for relative requests

select.cells.cells.name: cells
select.cells.cells.description:
Reactive state management with cells and derivations. Provides observable
data containers that notify subscribers on changes, enabling reactive UI
updates.
A `Cell` holds a value and notifies subscribers when it changes. A `Derived`
cell automatically updates when its source cells change. `Selected` provides
focused access to nested properties within cells.

examples.code:
```javascript
import { cell, derived } from "./select.cells.js"
const count = cell(0)
const doubled = derived(count, c => c * 2)
doubled.sub((value) => console.log("Doubled:", value))
count.set(5)  // Logs: "Doubled: 10"
```

select.cells.Selections.name: Selections
select.cells.Selections.description:
Internal registry for tracking reactive selections at specific paths.
Used by cells to notify nested property observers.

select.cells.Reactive.name: Reactive
select.cells.Reactive.description:
Base class for reactive values. Manages subscriptions, selections (nested
property observers), and provides generic collection API.

select.cells.Selected.name: Selected
select.cells.Selected.description:
A reactive view into a nested property of a parent cell. Updates when the
parent cell changes. Supports setting values which propagate to parent.

select.cells.Cell.name: Cell
select.cells.Cell.description:
A reactive container for a single value. Notifies subscribers on change.
Supports nested property updates via `set(value, path)`.

examples.code:
```javascript
const c = cell({ count: 0 })
c.sub((v) => console.log("Changed:", v))
c.set(1, "count")  // Updates nested property
```

select.cells.Deferred.name: Deferred
select.cells.Deferred.description:
A cell that debounces updates. `set()` triggers after `delay` ms of
inactivity. Useful for search inputs, sliders, etc.

select.cells.Derivation.name: Derivation
select.cells.Derivation.description:
A reactive value computed from a template of source cells. Automatically
updates and notifies when any source cell changes.

select.cells.cell.name: cell
select.cells.cell.description: Factory that creates a new Cell with optional initial value.

select.cells.deferred.name: deferred
select.cells.deferred.description: Factory that creates a new Deferred (debounced) cell.

select.cells.derived.name: derived
select.cells.derived.description: Factory that creates a new Derivation from template cells.

parameters.name: any
parameters.description: source shape (cell, list of cells, or map of cells)

select.cells.effect.name: effect
select.cells.effect.description:
Subscribes `effector` to all reactive values found in `inputs`.
Runs once immediately with expanded values, then on each source update.
Returns an idempotent disposer that unsubscribes all sources and runs the
latest cleanup returned by `effector`.

select.icons.icons.name: icons
select.icons.icons.description: SVG icon registry and loader utilities.

select.icons.parseIconName.name: parseIconName
select.icons.parseIconName.description:
Splits `name` into `[iconName, iconSource]`, preserving `source` as the
fallback when no source prefix is embedded in `name`.

select.icons.load.name: load
select.icons.load.description:
Loads the SVG symbol for `name` from `source`, appends it to `container`,
and returns a promise for the created ``.

select.icons.icon.name: icon
select.icons.icon.description:
Creates an SVG element for `name` using `source`. Returns either an inline
node or a `<use/></svg>` reference depending on `mode`.

select.icons.px.name: px
select.icons.px.description: Normalizes numeric size strings to CSS pixel values.

select.icons.IconElement.name: IconElement
select.icons.IconElement.description: Custom element that renders an icon from the shared registry.

select.icons.install.name: install
select.icons.install.description:
Registers the icon custom element under `name` and applies `options` to the
shared icon defaults.

select.select.name: select
select.select.description:
Factory function that creates a `Selection`. If `selector` is already a
Selection and no `scope` provided, returns it directly.

parameters.name: Selection
parameters.description: CSS selector, node(s), or existing Selection

select.match.name: match
select.match.description:
Tests if `node` matches the given CSS `selector`. Uses native browser
methods when available with fallback to manual parent query.

select.query.name: query
select.query.description:
Queries all descendants of `scope` matching `selector`. Wraps
`querySelectorAll` with support for direct child selectors (`>`) and
`:first` pseudo-class.

select.filter.name: filter
select.filter.description: Filters `nodes` array to only include elements matching `selector`.

select.Selection.name: Selection
select.Selection.description:
An array-like collection of DOM/SVG elements with chainable manipulation
methods. Extends native `Array`.

select.utils.utils.name: utils
select.utils.utils.description: Compatibility barrel for the split utility submodules.

select.index.index.name: index
select.index.index.description: Aggregates the core and companion modules into a single import surface.

select.browser.browser.name: browser
select.browser.browser.description:
Browser-backed reactive state for URL and local storage. The browser API
exposes `ref(value)` for reactive references, `val(value)` for plain value
coercion, and `parse(value)` as the compatibility dispatcher combining both.
